subject: RCC / Prestressed Concrete & Steel Structures
question: Pitch p shall not be more than

options.a: 16 t or 200 mm, whichever is less, in tension members
options.b: 12 t or 200 mm, whichever is less, is case of comp. members.
options.c: In case of staggered pitch, pitch may be increased by 50% provided gauge distance is less

subject: RCC / Prestressed Concrete & Steel Structures
question: The following are the statements about lug angle used to connected heavily loaded tension member to gusset plates. (i) The length of end connection is reduced (ii) By using lug angles there will be saving in the gusset plate (iii) Cost of connection increases due to additional fasteners and angle required

options.c: only (ii) and (iii) are correct.

subject: RCC / Prestressed Concrete & Steel Structures
question: Which one of the following is not a correct statement about lug angle connection?

options.a: By using lug angle, there will be saving in gusset plate.
options.b: The connection of lug angle to main member shall preferably start in advance of the member to
options.c: Minimum of two bolts are used for connecting lug angle to the gusset plate.
options.d: Maximum of five bolts should be used in the connection.

subject: RCC / Prestressed Concrete & Steel Structures
question: A structural member subjected to tensile force in the direction parallel to its longitudinal axis, is called

options.c: a tie

subject: RCC / Prestressed Concrete & Steel Structures
question: Which of the following statement is correct ?

options.a: The term tie is commonly used for tension
options.b: When a tension member is subjected to axial
options.c: A tension member subjected to axial tension is

subject: RCC / Prestressed Concrete & Steel Structures
question: The tension members are used as hangers for floors.

subject: RCC / Prestressed Concrete & Steel Structures
question: The single channel sections, used as tension member, have

options.a: low rigidity in the direction of web and high
options.b: high rigidity in the direction of web and low
options.c: equal rigidity in the direction of web and flange

subject: RCC / Prestressed Concrete & Steel Structures
question: The net sectional area of a tension member is equal to

options.c: gross-sectional area plus the maximum deduction

subject: RCC / Prestressed Concrete & Steel Structures
question: When a tension member is made of four angles with a plate as web, the allowance for holes is made as

options.a: two holes for each angle and one hole for the web
options.b: one hole for each angle and one hole for the web
options.c: one hole for each angle and two holes for the web

subject: RCC / Prestressed Concrete & Steel Structures
question: A tension member subjected to axial tension is designed on the basis of its

options.c: gross-sectional area

subject: RCC / Prestressed Concrete & Steel Structures
question: In order to determine the gross-sectional area of a tension member consisting of two angle sections, the allowance for holes is made as

options.c: three holes for each angle

subject: RCC / Prestressed Concrete & Steel Structures
question: In order to determine the gross-sectional area of a tension member consisting of two laced channels, the allowance for holes is made as

options.c: two holes from each channel web or one hole
